mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and pushing S&P 500 forward returns positive over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from cyclical construction to durable goods manufacturing, indicating resilient aggregate demand that supports equity valuations and yields positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising real rates compress equity multiples, but if real GDP growth remains above 2.5 percent, earnings resilience offsets valuation compression, resulting in neutral to positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn significantly negative when FEDFUNDS rises and GDPC1 exceeds 2.5%, or the combined signal's predictive power vanishes in a rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding business hiring cycles, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of consecutive monthly PAYEMS increase episodes, or the rolling 1-year directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.0 percent annualized growth rate, inflation expectations embed into discount rates, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth surpasses 3.0%, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where consumer credit outstanding declines while the federal funds rate rises signals tightening financial conditions that restrict household spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling RSAFS and rising FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained environment where industrial production growth slows below 1.0 percent, persistent labor market strength forces firms to absorb higher wage costs without passing them to prices, compressing margins and driving negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth rate falls below 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO growth drops below 0.5% while PAYEMS rises, or the margin compression signal fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances.
